skill.name: market-access-value
skill.description: Generates structured pharmacoeconomic value propositions for pharmaceutical market access, including ICER calculations, budget impact models, and payer-facing value dossier sections.
skill.summary: Generate payer-facing value propositions for drug market access with structured ICER calculations and budget impact narratives.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: Static analyzer flagged 41 patterns, but after manual evaluation, the overwhelming majority are false positives. The 'backtick execution' detections are markdown code formatting in .md files, 'weak crypto' detections hit on argparse and documentation text, 'path traversal' detections hit on security checklist items warning against traversal, and 'system reconnaissance' detections hit on administrative audit content. The only confirmed finding is a file write at scripts/main.py:88 via user-controlled argparse output path, which is legitimate CLI functionality. No network access, no credential handling, no code injection, and no prompt injection attempts were found. Risk level is low.

low_findings.title: File Write to User-Controlled Output Path
low_findings.description: The --output CLI argument in scripts/main.py allows writing to any filesystem path specified by the user. No path validation or sandboxing is enforced. This is by design for a CLI tool but represents a filesystem modification capability with no boundary restrictions.

low_findings.title: Documentation-Only Hardcoded URLs
low_findings.description: SKILL.md line 7 contains a hardcoded GitHub URL pointing to the skill's source repository. This is standard documentation linking with no network calls originating from the code. Not a security risk.

dangerous_patterns.title: User-Specified File Output via argparse
dangerous_patterns.description: The script accepts --output/-o argument and writes generated content to that path via open(args.output, 'w'). This gives the user full control over the output destination with no path validation.

dangerous_patterns.title: Markdown Backtick False Positives (Static Analyzer Noise)
dangerous_patterns.description: The static analyzer flagged 27 backtick occurrences across SKILL.md and references/audit-reference.md as 'Ruby/shell backtick execution'. These are all markdown inline code formatting or fenced code blocks in documentation files, not executable backtick syntax. Verified as false positives.

content.user_title: Generate Market Access Value Propositions for Pharma
content.value_statement: Creates structured payer-facing value propositions for pharmaceutical market access. Helps researchers develop ICER calculations, budget impact models, and value dossier sections for HTA submissions and payer negotiations.
actual_capabilities: Generate structured pharmacoeconomic value propositions with clinical, economic, and comparative effectiveness sections
Document cost-effectiveness metrics including cost per QALY and budget impact narratives
Produce patient-centered outcomes analysis with quality of life assessment frameworks
Create payer-specific value dossier sections with objection handling templates
Support HTA submission documentation with structured assumptions and explicit risk identification
Generate ICER calculation narratives with uncertainty analysis placeholders
limitations: Does not perform actual statistical analysis or health economic modeling computations
Requires user-provided clinical and economic data inputs for all sections
Cannot access real-world drug pricing databases, formulary information, or external data sources
Output quality depends entirely on the completeness and accuracy of user-provided inputs

use_cases.title: HTA Submission Preparation
use_cases.description: Health economists preparing drug reimbursement submissions can generate structured value dossiers with ICER calculations and budget impact narratives for health technology assessment bodies.

use_cases.title: Payer Negotiation Support
use_cases.description: Market access teams can create evidence-based value propositions to support pricing and reimbursement negotiations with public and private payers across different markets.

use_cases.title: Academic Pharmacoeconomic Writing
use_cases.description: Researchers can produce structured pharmacoeconomic manuscript sections and grant application materials with explicit assumptions and reproducible output formats.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Value Proposition
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a market access value proposition for [drug name] indicated for [condition]. Include clinical efficacy, safety profile, and economic value sections with cost-effectiveness analysis.
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick start for generating a standard value proposition document

prompt_templates.title: ICER-Focused Cost-Effectiveness Analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: Calculate and present the cost-effectiveness of [drug] compared to [comparator] for [indication]. Include QALY calculations, budget impact estimates over 3 years, and uncertainty analysis with sensitivity ranges.
prompt_templates.scenario: Detailed incremental cost-effectiveness ratio analysis for payer submissions

prompt_templates.title: Targeted Payer Value Dossier
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a payer value dossier section for [drug] targeting [specific market, e.g., US commercial payers]. Address clinical differentiation from standard of care, economic burden of disease, and budget impact projections.
prompt_templates.scenario: Market-specific payer dossier with tailored value messaging

prompt_templates.title: Complete HTA Submission Package
prompt_templates.prompt: Prepare a complete HTA submission value package for [drug] in [indication]. Include: product overview, clinical value summary with key trial data, cost-effectiveness analysis with ICER, budget impact model narrative, patient-reported outcomes, and payer objection responses.
prompt_templates.scenario: Comprehensive submission package for health technology assessment review

output_examples.input: Generate a value proposition for a new oncology drug targeting non-small cell lung cancer with improved progression-free survival
output_examples.output: A structured document containing product overview with drug name and mechanism, clinical value section with efficacy and safety data, economic value with cost per QALY estimates, comparative effectiveness analysis versus platinum-based chemotherapy, and patient-centered outcomes with quality of life improvements.

output_examples.input: Calculate the ICER for Drug X compared to adalimumab in rheumatoid arthritis with a budget impact analysis
output_examples.output: An ICER calculation narrative showing the incremental cost-effectiveness ratio, budget impact projections over a 1 to 5 year horizon, cost offset analysis from reduced hospitalizations and improved productivity, and uncertainty analysis with one-way and probabilistic sensitivity ranges.

best_practices: Provide complete clinical and economic data inputs before running the skill to avoid partial or placeholder-filled outputs
Review and validate all generated assumptions against actual clinical trial data and published evidence before submission
Use the generated output as a structured first draft and customize payer-specific messaging for each target market and audience
anti_patterns: Do not submit generated outputs to regulators or payers without expert review of clinical and economic accuracy
Avoid using placeholder data in final submissions without replacing every placeholder with actual trial or real-world evidence results
Do not treat generated ICER calculations and budget impact narratives as validated health economic models without independent verification

faq.question: What data inputs does this skill require?
faq.answer: The skill requires drug name, indication, efficacy data, safety profile, comparator information, cost-effectiveness metrics, and target market details.

faq.question: Can this skill perform actual statistical health economic modeling?
faq.answer: No. The skill generates structured narratives and document frameworks. You must provide your own modeled data and calculations.

faq.question: What output formats does the skill produce?
faq.answer: The skill produces structured text documents with sections for clinical value, economic value, comparative effectiveness, and patient-centered outcomes.

faq.question: Is the output suitable for direct regulatory submission?
faq.answer: No. The output is a structured draft framework. Expert review, customization, and validation are required before regulatory or payer submission.

faq.question: Which healthcare markets does the skill support?
faq.answer: The skill can structure value propositions for US, EU, and Japan market access contexts based on user-provided market parameters and pricing data.

faq.question: Does the skill connect to external databases or pricing sources?
faq.answer: No. All economic and clinical data must be provided by the user. The skill does not make any network calls or access external databases.
